intro: Hedyotis insularis, also known as Islay gentian, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, Hedyotis insularis is used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to treat fever, cough, and sore throat, as well as to boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis insularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant is often used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
